title: Council Outcome
description: One council run: the user's task, the providers it dispatched to, what each said, the chairman's structured synthesis, and the user verdict (if recorded). Lives at ~/.trinity/council_outcomes/<council_run_id>.json. The canonical store for cross-provider preference signal — everything downstream (personal_routing_table, picks.json, lens.md) is computed from this.

council_run_id.description: Stable id, sha1 prefix. Used as the on-disk filename.

bundle_id.description: Id of the originating prompt bundle (the user's question + context).

task_cluster_id.description: Optional basin/topic id this council belongs to. Used for per-basin aggregation downstream.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC. When the council started.

primary_provider.description: The chairman provider (the model that ran the synthesis pass).

primary_session_id.description: Session ID of the chairman provider's underlying call. Lets the harness correlate a council with the provider's own session record (e.g. a Claude Code session uuid). Optional.

agreement_score.description: Aggregate cross-member agreement scalar [0.0, 1.0]. Higher = more consensus on agreed_claims. Lets downstream consumers (replay, ranking) filter by deliberation quality without re-parsing routing_label.disagreed_claims.

needs_followup.description: Chairman-flagged signal that the council should be re-run with refined context or different members (e.g. all members hedged, the question was misframed, etc.). Downstream consumers can treat as a soft escalation hint.

winner_provider.description: Chairman's pick of which member's answer was strongest. This IS the supervision signal — feeds compute_personal_routing_table() directly.

member_results.description: One entry per member that responded. Order is provider name; check `provider` field.

session_id.description: Provider-side session token (Claude Code session, Codex CLI session, etc.) if the dispatch retained one.

metadata.description: Per-member free-form metadata (timing, retry counts, fallback signals) — keys are stable but contents evolve. Empty {} when nothing notable.

synthesis_prompt.description: The full prompt the chairman saw. Includes member outputs and the user's lens.md.

synthesis_output.description: Chairman's narrative synthesis — the user-facing answer.

differences.description: Free-text summary OR a list of difference points. Legacy field; downstream consumers should prefer `routing_label.disagreed_claims` which carries the structured shape.

routing_label.description: Structured JSON the chairman emits per council. This is the supervision signal Trinity learns from.

mode.description: Council shape. `parallel` (default) runs members concurrently then chairman synthesizes. `chain` runs sequential refinement. `consensus_round` is a legacy/pre-v1 shape preserved for historical outcomes; new code emits parallel or chain only.

chain_steps.description: Populated when mode=chain. One entry per sequential refinement step.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the step's dispatch began. Empty string when timing wasn't captured.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the step's dispatch finished.

metadata.description: Per-step free-form metadata (retry count, fallback signals). Omitted when empty.

Free-form sidecar. Notable key: chain_root_id (thread grouping). The legacy user_verdict block was retired 2026-05-22 along with the entire rating surface — chairman's winner is the supervision signal now (compute_personal_routing_table aggregates from routing_label.winner directly). Existing on-disk councils get user_verdict wiped on next read by load_council_outcome.

chain_root_id.description: Groups multi-round chain councils as one thread. All councils sharing chain_root_id are refinement steps of the same original question.

routing_label.description: Structured Routing JSON the chairman emits per council. The supervision-signal shape every downstream consumer reads.

task_type.description: Chairman-labeled task kind (e.g. 'coding', 'design', 'writing'). Distinct from `category` (the coarser LMArena-aligned grouping).

winner.description: Chairman's pick — duplicates the top-level `winner_provider`, kept here for self-contained routing-label parsing.

confidence.description: Chairman's confidence in the winner. Numeric in [0, 1] or a categorical label ('low' | 'medium' | 'high') — the chairman emits whichever shape the synthesis prompt called for.

agreed_claims.description: Statements all members converged on. The compressed signal.

disagreed_claims.description: Statements where members diverged + why it matters. The residual signal (see meta-principle #18). Each entry: {claim, why_matters, providers[]}.

provider_scores.description: Per-provider scores on this task. Two shapes are valid: (1) flat `{provider: number}` for a single composite score; (2) nested `{provider: {axis: score}}` when the chairman emits a per-axis rubric (overall / planning / execution / specificity / user_fit / risk / conciseness etc.). Downstream consumers normalize to the composite when aggregating.

user_likely_values.description: Inferred user-preference axes. Folds into lens.md over time.

routing_lesson.description: One-sentence takeaway for the personal_routing_table — why this winner won.

major_failure_mode.description: What the losing member(s) got wrong, in user-facing terms.

eval_seed.description: Optional seed-prompt label, used by the corpus-based eval harness (#122) to slice replayable benchmarks.
